title: Linker error when running SV testbench  Scrt1.o: in function `_start':
body:
Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)
The code is available here
https://github.com/rishubn/ascon-verilog-sca/tree/main
With the command: 
```
verilator --cc --top tb --timing --trace-fst -Wno-unoptflat -Wno-widthexpand -Wno-latch -Wno-implicit -Wno-pinmissing -Wno-timescalemod -Irtl rtl/asconp.sv rtl/ascon_core.sv rtl/tb.sv --exe --build -j
```

The error:

```
make: Entering directory '/home/rnagpal/src/ascon-verilog-sca/obj_dir'
g++     verilated.o verilated_fst_c.o verilated_timing.o verilated_threads.o Vtb__ALL.a   -lz  -pthread -lpthread -latomic   -o Vtb
/usr/bin/ld: /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/11/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/Scrt1.o: in function `_start':
(.text+0x1b): undefined reference to `main'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make: *** [Vtb.mk:62: Vtb] Error 1
```



What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
```
Verilator 5.016 2023-09-16 rev v5.014-149-g57c816f90
```
What OS and distribution are you using?
Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
code_blocks:
make: Entering directory '/home/rnagpal/src/ascon-verilog-sca/obj_dir'
g++     verilated.o verilated_fst_c.o verilated_timing.o verilated_threads.o Vtb__ALL.a   -lz  -pthread -lpthread -latomic   -o Vtb
/usr/bin/ld: /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/11/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/Scrt1.o: in function `_start':
(.text+0x1b): undefined reference to `main'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make: *** [Vtb.mk:62: Vtb] Error 1
